questions.question: Explain the concept of demand paging in virtual memory systems. How does it differ from pure paging?
keyPoints: Reduction in I/O and memory requirements

questions.question: Consider a memory with 3 frames and the following page reference string: 1,2,3,4,1,2,5,1,2,3,4,5. Using the FIFO page replacement algorithm, calculate the number of page faults. Show your work.
keyPoints: FIFO replaces oldest page in memory
Count page faults when page not in memory

questions.question: In a demand paging system, memory access time is 200 ns and average page fault service time is 10 ms. If the page fault rate is 0.05, calculate the effective memory access time.
keyPoints: Formula: EAT = (1-p) × memory access + p × page fault service
Convert units to same scale (ms or ns)

questions.question: What is thrashing in virtual memory systems? Explain its causes and effects, and suggest possible solutions to prevent thrashing.
keyPoints: Caused by insufficient frames for active processes
